# Transcrição: How_to_speak_with_MORE_clarity

- **Fonte:** How_to_speak_with_MORE_clarity
- **Duração:** 01:08
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:08]

Paul says, Hey then, when I present in sales, I end up talking really fast, which causes me to mumble. The simplest thing you can do, Paul, be conscious of moving your mouth more when you speak. Right now, I can speak really quickly if I wanted to, and I would still sound rather clear to you. And the reason why I sound really clear, even though I'm speaking really quickly, is because I'm moving my mouth a lot more. I'm moving my tongue a lot more. To do this, we generally have to practice, and the best way to practice is tongue twisters. 6, 6, Hicks, Nick, 6, Slip Bricks. So think of rate of speech as having a scale of one to ten. One being really slow. That's how you start reading the tongue twister at the start, and you'll get them all with 100% accuracy. So you start at level one, and then you move to level two. You'll continue to get them correct, but why do you do it slow and then fast? Why don't you just immediately try to do it fast? Because when you do it slow, what you're doing there is you're learning the mouth movements for each and every single word. And once you learn the mouth movements, only then can you speed up the process. Whereas if you're trying to do speed initially at the start, it means you're forgoing learning the mouth movements, which means you're just making it harder for yourself.
